def: s.m. Head-place, head-part, head (of a bedstead, or tomb, &c.); a pillow
more: s. m. The head part or side of any thing (as, of a tomb or bedstead), the place where the head rests or reclines.
subs: adv. At the head of (a bed, &c.).

def: n. m. S. शिरोधारण The head of a bedstead or tomb; a pillow. sirhā'ne , adv. At the head of bed.
s.f. A stair, step; a ladder; a degree; — sīṛhiyāṅ, s.f. pl. A flight of steps
more: s. f. A ladder, stair, step; degree.
subs: To rise, or climb, step by step
'To put up a ladder and climb,' to mount or climb by means of a ladder.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀟𑁆𑀠𑀺 (siḍḍhi), from Sanskrit *श्रीढि (*śrīḍhi).

def: n. f. S. एढ़ी 1. A ladder (زينه). 2. A step ( paiṛī ). sīṛhiyāṅ , n. m. 1. A flight of steps. Yeh Jāme-masjid kī sīṛhiyoṅ ke baiṭhne-vāle haiṅ . This fellow is one of those who sit on the steps of the Jāmā-masjid. (The daily resort of reprobates in Delhi). 2. Step; degree ( darjah ). sīṛhī sīṛhī chaṛhnā , v. a. To rise step by step sīṛhī kā ḍanḍā . A step or rung of a ladder. sīṛhī lagā-ke chaṛhnā , v. a. To climb by means of a ladder.
s. f. (dakh.) See سيڙهي sīṛhī.
s.m. A plough-holder (an epithet of Balārām); — a sharer, partner (see sīr).
s.f. corr. of sīṛhī, q.v. s.f. A stair, step; a ladder; a degree; — sīṛhiyāṅ, s.f. pl. A flight of steps
subs: To rise, or climb, step by step
'To put up a ladder and climb,' to mount or climb by means of a ladder.

def: s.f. The head (esp. when severed from the trunk).
more: n. f.A goat, sheep's, or kid's head. sirī pāë . Sheep's head and trotters. ( Butchers ). bin-sirā , n. m. A headless demon. be-sirā, be-sirī , adj. 1. Without a head or director. 2. Headstrong ( khud-sar ). do sir ikhaṭṭe k., yā joṛnā , v. a. To join two heads together. 2. To tie the nuptial knot. Do sir ikhaṭṭe karne meṅ baṛā savāb hai. lākh yā sau sir kā h. , v. n. To be headstrong, obstinate. Bachchā lākh sir kā ho gayā . Wom. 2. To be deaf to all reasons. 3. To give a great many reasons. Koī lākh sir kā ho-kar āë to bhī maiṅ na mānūṅ. Wom. maile sir se honā , v. n. To have the menses.

def: adj. & s.m. Mad, insane, crazy; — a madman ( = siṛā).
more: adj. m. Mad. See سري sirrī.
s. (dakh.) Ladder ( Harris ): see سيڙهي sīṛhī.

def: adj. = siṛī or siṛṛī, q.v. s.f. The head (esp. when severed from the trunk).
s.m. The tree Acacia (or Mimosa) sirisa; and its flower.
more: s. m. The name of a tree. (Mimosa sirisa).

def: s.f. (?) A kind of muslin.
more: n. A kind of muslin. Sarīsāf, chautārā pahnā, pahnā malmal khāsā; Ek din hogā terā, bhāī, aṅt khāk māṅ bāsā . Muslins and velvet fine you may display, But one day, brother! in the dust you'll lay.

def: s.m. = siris, q.v. s.m. The tree Acacia (or Mimosa) sirisa; and its flower.
s.m. = siris, q.v. s.m. The tree Acacia (or Mimosa) sirisa; and its flower.
more: prop. sirīsh, s. m. Glue, starch.

def: s.m. The tree Acacia (or Mimosa) sirisa, and its flower.
s.m. = saresh, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Glue ( Harris ): see سريش saresh.

def: s.m. Glue; starch.

ety: Pārsī śriśk; Pehl. śrishk; Zend śraśka, rt. śraść; cf. S. अश्रु
def: s.m. Tear, tear-drop.
more: Poet. n. m. A tear ( āṅsū ).
prop. sirishk, s. m. A tear.

ety: = S. सृष्टि; prob. fr. Zend śarj, rt. śar
def: s.f. Nature, temperament, constitution, disposition, complexion; — natural shape, form, make; — intellect, understanding.
more: n. f. P. sarisht . The creation; world.
prop. sirisht (Sans. सृष्टि ) s. f. 1. Nature, temperament, constitution, disposition, complexion. 2. Intellect.
ex: آواز اونچی کر کے بولنا میری سرشت میں نہیں
āvāz ū̃ci kar ke bolnā merī sariśt mẽ nahī̃
Speaking in a loud voice is not in my nature
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرشت (sirišt).

ety: perf. part. of sirishtan, fr. śarj, rt. Zend śar; and = S. सृष्ट, rt. सृज्
def: part. Mixed; created, made.
more: s. m. See سررشته. ¶ sirishta, pass. part. (of سرشتن) Mixed, created.

def: s.m. corr. of sarrishtadār, q.v.s.v. sarrishta.
s.m. The province of Syria.
s.m. = سرکه sirka, q.v.
s.m. Vinegar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِرْکَه (sirka).

To bend down the head; to bow, nod; to be obedfent or submissive, to submit; to be humbled or humiliated
v.t. To lay on the head (of another, -ke)

To talk under the influence (of an evil spirit, &c.)

To turn (on or upon), to attack

A Mah. corpse borne on a bed by four men. What has, pray tell, five heads, four lives, ten arms, and legs fourteen, This riddle solve, or in this village never more be seen! 2. A female on a female's head, She waits her husband homeward led, All night she weeps, powerless her will, And morning sees her weeping still.

ety: Pehl. śiyāh, śyāk; Zend śyāva; S. श्याम
def: adj. Black, dark, sombre; unfortunate, bad
more: adj. S. श्याम Black; sable; sombre; dark. Syāh-fām . Black-complexioned. siyāh-bātin , n. m. A dissembler; hypocrite. siyāh-bakht . Unfortunate; unlucky. siyāh-bakhtī , n. f. Misfortune ( bad-bakhtī ). siyāh-posh , adj. Dressed in black or mourning. siyāh-tāb , adj. Shining-black. Pahne hue jāma-i-siyāh tāb, Phirtā thā woh shab meṅ jaise mahtāb. siyāh-tālū , adj. Black-mouthed (a horse). siyāh-chashm , adj. Black-eyed. siyāh-dil , adj. Black-hearted. siyāh-rū , adj. 1. Black-faced. 2. Disgraced. siyāh-kār , adj. Sinful; wicked; bad. siyāh-kārī , n. f. Black deeds; deeds of darkness; foul deeds; wickedness. siyāh k . v. a. 1. To blacken. 2. To write badly; scribble. 3. To mar; spoil; undo. Siyāh karo, chāhe safaid karo . Prov. Saying. Or blacken it or make it white. (Do as you like). siyāh-gosh , n. m. An animal; Felis caracal . (?). siyāh-mast , adj. Dead-drunk. siyāh-mastī , n. f. Exces…

adj. Black; unfortunate, bad. سياه بخت siyāh-baḵẖt, adj. Unfortunate. سياه بختي siyāh-baḵẖt-ī, s. f. Misfortune. سياه پوش siyāh-posh, Clad in black. سياه تالو siyāh-tālū, adj. Black-mouthed (horse), abusive, whose curses prevail. سياه چشم siyāh-chashm, adj. 1. Black-eyed. 2. Unkind. سياه چشمي siyāh-chashm-ī, s. f. Black-eyedness. سياه دل siyāh-dil, adj. Black-hearted; malevolent. سياه رو siyāh-rū, adj. Black-faced; unlucky, disgraced. سياه روز siyāh-roz, adj. Unfortunate. سياه روزي siyāh-roz-ī, Adverse fortune. سياه طالعي siyāh-t̤āliʼīﻌ, s. f. Misfortune, bad luck. سياه فام siyāh-fām, adj. Black. سياه قلم کرنا siyāh-ḳalam karnā, To delineate. سياه کار siyāh-kār, adj. Wicked, tyrannical, cheat, sinner. سياه کاري siyāh-kār-ī, s. f. Wickedness, misfortune. سياه کامه siyāh-kāsa, adj. Miser, niggard; a sordid wretch. سياه کرنا siyāh karnā, To take an account of. سياه مست siyāh-mast, adj. Dead…
subs: adj. & s.m. Black-hearted, false-hearted; — a false-hearted man, dissembler, hypocrite
adj. Clad in black, dressed in mourning; — one who is clothed in black
adj. Black and shining, glossy black
adj. Black-mouthed (a horse, &c.); abusive; whose curses prevail
adj. Black-faced; unlucky; disgraced; — s.m. One whose face is black
adj. & s.m. Sinful, wicked, bad; tyrannical; — evil-doer, sinner; cheat

s.m. lit. 'Black cup'; a miser, niggard, sordid wretch

s.m. lit. 'Black-eared'; a lynx, Felix caracal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سیاه (siyāh), from Avestan 𐬯𐬌𐬌𐬁𐬎𐬎𐬀 (siiāuua), ultimately from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćyaHwás. Cognate with Sanskrit श्याव (śyāva), Russian сивый (sivyj, “grey”), Lithuanian šývas (“light grey”), Old English hīew (whence English hue).

def: s.f. Management, conduct (of affairs), right ordering, rule, government, governance, administration; jurisdiction, legal authority; — correction, punishment, chastisement; torture, pain, pang, agony; severity, rigour
more: n. f. سوس managed, ruled. 1. Jurisdiction; legal authority; government; administration; management. 2. Correction ( ḍaṅḍ , 4). 3. Severity; rigour. siyāsat k. , v. a. 1. To administer. 2. To chastise. siyāsat ke tābe . Amenable to jurisdiction. siyāsat-i-mudanī . The science of Government.

(from سوس) s. f. 1. Government, administration of justice. 2. Punishment, pain, chastisement, pang, agony. سياست کرنا siyāsat karnā, To chastise, to punish. سياست مين هونا siyāsat meṅ honā, To be in pain.
subs: (-kī), To manage; to rule, govern, &c.; to chastise, &c.

To be in torture, or agony, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِیَاسَت (siyāsat), from Arabic سِيَاسَة (siyāsa). Compare Sindhi سياست (syāst).

(This seems to be a catalogue of trees; it also means) God has been kind to you; that which you desired is obtained; adore his footsteps; silly woman! leave not your lover for a moment.

def: s.m. Clinging to, embracing; an embrace; union, junction, contact, proximity; association, society, presence; a paronomasia, double entendre, equivoque, pun, irony.
more: n. m. A pun; an equivoque, e. g. 1. Kīkar, pākar, tār, jāman, phalsā, āmalā, Sev, kadam, kachnār, pīpal, rattī, tūn, taj! Acacia, and myrobalan, rose-apple, fālsā , palm, and fig, The toon, kachnār , and pīpal, kadam , apple, rattī , laurel twig. 2. Kī kirpā Kartār, jā, man, phal sā ā milā, Sev kadam, kachi nār, pī pal, rattī tu na taj! God's goodness shewn to you, the fruit thereof content receive, Worship his feet, O maid unripe! spouse cherish ne'er to leave!
